sourceMovie.title: Parallels
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Parallels (2015) stuck because it commits to its science fiction premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Christopher Leone's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: Solo: A Star Wars Story
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Parallels clicked for you, Solo: A Star Wars Story (2018) is a strong next stop: both trade in science fiction, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Parallels?
faq.answer: Try Solo: A Star Wars Story, Valerian and the City of a Thousand Planets, Spectral—each shares DNA with Parallels while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Parallels?
faq.answer: Parallels (2015) is best described as Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Parallels worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this science fiction lane.
